body.title: CLOSED: Librarian health aggregator
body.result: HealthAggregator+rack_health MCP tool, 15 tests, silence thresholds (c4d7b22)
body.description:
The Librarian sits in IMAP IDLE on comms://heartbeat and collects heartbeat envelopes from all rack devices. Maintains a last-seen table: device_id → timestamp.

Silence detection:
  > 2N seconds — log warning, mark device suspect
  > 3N seconds — mark device down, expose via health summary MCP tool

Exposes via MCP: rack_health() tool returning per-device last-seen + status. CC can call this instead of querying each device individually.

current_action field from heartbeat gives 'what is it doing right now' for free — addresses the observability gap where only 'is it alive' was knowable.

**Affected files:** agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/health_aggregator.py, agent_datacenter/devices/librarian/mcp_server.py
**Design rules:** no-sqlite; last-seen table is in-memory (rack restarts are clean); gate on T-bus-heartbeat and T-bus-imap-idle-client
**Scope boundary:** aggregation + MCP exposure only; no alerting/paging in this ticket
**Test plan:** heartbeat from device A updates last-seen; silence > 3N marks A down; rack_health() MCP tool returns correct per-device status

**Warm-up clause (audit amendment):** after Librarian restart the last-seen table is empty. Do not flag silence for the first 2N seconds — allow the table to repopulate before silence thresholds activate.
